tools.name: ImageDescription

tools.name: GoogleSearch
tools.description: Searches the web for brief reference information to interpret domain-specific clinical staging terms.

inputs.name: k
inputs.description: Number of results or snippets to return

dialogs.content: What is the purpose of staging in this context?

dialogs.thought: First orient to the figure type and overall content, since it appears to be a cohort flowchart summarizing diagnostic and staging branches.

content.content: A black-and-white flowchart/tree diagram labeled “Study group n=166” that branches by surgical histology (hist− vs hist+), then by nodal stage (stage 1, stage 2, stage 3), then by ultrasound node status (US node+ vs US node−), and then by whether ultrasound-guided biopsy was performed (USB vs no USB) with terminal counts including M, B, and SE categories.

dialogs.thought: Extract the labels to confirm that the diagram summarizes surgical histology, nodal stage, ultrasound visibility, and ultrasound-guided core biopsy outcomes with patient counts.

arguments.query: What is the purpose of nodal staging involving ultrasound core biopsy outcomes and in what stage it is useful?

dialogs.thought: Clarify what staging refers to in this diagram and what clinical purpose it serves, so the figure purpose can be explained in terms of nodal staging and biopsy triage.

content.content: Nodal staging is used to estimate the extent of lymph-node involvement, guide treatment planning, and help decide whether patients should proceed to further axillary surgery or other management. Ultrasound with needle or core biopsy is especially useful preoperatively when suspicious nodes are seen, helping identify patients with higher-stage nodal disease before surgery.

arguments.attribute: what clinical variables and outcomes are being stratified and counted in this region

dialogs.thought: Verify in the main branching region that the figure links surgical nodal stage with ultrasound node status and biopsy outcomes, which explains why the staging summary is shown.

content.content: The region shows a branching cohort diagram with patient counts stratified by nodal stage at surgery (stage 1/2/3), ultrasound node status (US node+ vs US node−), whether ultrasound-guided biopsy was done (USB vs no USB), and terminal biopsy outcome categories labeled M, B, and SE with corresponding n values.

dialogs.content: The purpose of the figure is to summarize how patients in the study are distributed by surgical histology, nodal stage, ultrasound node status, and ultrasound-guided core biopsy outcomes. It is shown to illustrate how preoperative ultrasound and biopsy findings relate to nodal staging at surgery, helping assess how useful ultrasound-guided biopsy is for identifying patients with nodal involvement before surgery and for treatment planning.

whitelist: The purpose of the figure is to summarize how patients in the study are distributed by surgical histology, nodal stage, ultrasound node status, and ultrasound-guided core biopsy outcomes. It is shown to illustrate how preoperative ultrasound and biopsy findings relate to nodal staging at surgery, helping assess how useful ultrasound-guided biopsy is for identifying patients with nodal involvement before surgery and for treatment planning.
